title: Birth rate, crude (per 1,000 people)
definition: Crude birth rate indicates the number of live births occurring during the year, per 1,000 population estimated at midyear. Subtracting the crude death rate from the crude birth rate provides the rate of natural increase, which is equal to the rate of population change in the absence of migration.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata

editorial.shortHook: The broadest signal of where populations are adding children.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Central African Republic, Chad, Somalia, Fed. Rep., while the lightest include Korea, Rep., Ukraine, Japan; that contrast is the fastest way to read the birth rate pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Birth rates give a fast read on population momentum without requiring population totals.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Population ages 0-14 can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Fertility rate can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Under-5 mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Intro difficulty: the map has broad, recognizable regional anchors and a player-facing unit that is easy to reason about.
regionalSignals: Age structure often makes the map readable before labels do.

reviewNotes: Too close to fertility rate and children-share for Daily.
Still useful as a Practice comparison map for demographic transition.
